digest.component_or_area: operation-cert signing / attestation subsystem (plan-review + completion-verifier gate verdicts)
propositions: Operation certificates are migrated from per-clone symmetric HMAC self-attestations to asymmetric Ed25519 signatures verifiable by any third party against the signing environment's public key.

Each certificate is attributed to the signing environment under namespace rebar.opcert.v1, and a project may optionally require a certificate from a specific trusted environment for a gate while low-security projects keep fast local-report behavior.

Trusted-environment public keys are pinned out-of-band in .rebar/trusted_environments.yaml on the Gerrit-gated code branch (never the auto-pushed tickets branch), with era validity via explicit main-commit SHAs compared using git merge-base --is-ancestor, reusing the identity epic's era-validity rule and key_not_valid_at_era verdict.

A trusted rebar-mcp remote-execution path must fetch authoritative ticket state and code-at-commit itself and run the gate on that, so a developer cannot forge a passing verdict from client-supplied inputs; certificates bind {ticket id, material fingerprint, merged-log commit SHA} to prevent replay.

The legacy HMAC op-cert scheme is removed via an expand-contract migration so no code path signs or accepts HMAC op-certs; gate producers mint op-certs with the ambient environment key and the merge gate recomputes/constrains verified values rather than trusting the stored record.

The trusted signing service runs as a compose service on the existing rebar-gerrit box behind an API Gateway HTTP API with IAM SigV4 auth and an Ed25519 key in SSM SecureString, achieving zero fixed-monthly-cost increase, while the default local path stays offline and never wedges an operation.
